fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 12-year-old female presents after conflict with best friend at school.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 12-year-old female brought by her mother following an argument with her best friend yesterday that resulted in the patient crying in the school counselor's office. She reports feeling upset and betrayed after her friend shared a secret with other classmates. She endorses feeling sad and hurt but denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states she has been unable to sleep well for the past night but maintains appetite. She remains interested in her art projects and is looking forward to her dance recital next week. Mother describes patient as generally resilient and notes she has successfully navigated peer conflicts in the past. (Social Worker Laura Brennan, LCSW)
fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Adjustment disorder with mixed emotional features, mild.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents and older brother; strong family support; engaged in multiple extracurricular activities; generally good peer relationships.
Assessment_and_Plan: Acute distress related to peer conflict.
Protective factors: strong family support, good coping history, engaged in creative activities.
School counselor involvement for peer mediation recommended.
Resources for continued outpatient counseling provided if needed.
fields.Author: Laura Brennan, LCSW (Social Worker)
